name: Bobby Capó
abstract: Félix Manuel "Bobby" Rodríguez Capó (January 1, 1922 – December 18, 1989) was a Puerto Rican singer and songwriter. He usually combined ballads with classical music and was deeply involved in Puerto Rican folk elements and even Andalusian music, as to produce many memorable Latino pop songs which featured elaborate, dramatic lyrics.

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: So there I accompanied . . . yes, many people of the great artists.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: we was at La Conga with Bert Longworth and when he got there, Miguelito Valdes and Diosa Costelo and Bobby Capo were .... Were in the same bill with you? ... Yes; and Miguelito and Diosa and Bobby Capo.
